example_sentence: El libro que me regalaste es increíble.
example_translation: The book you gave me is incredible.

translation: who / whom (for people, especially after prepositions)
example_sentence: La maestra con quien estudié es de Veracruz.
example_translation: The teacher with whom I studied is from Veracruz.

example_sentence: La ciudad donde nací tiene muchos volcanes.
example_translation: The city where I was born has many volcanoes.

example_sentence: Lo que más me gusta de México es la gente.
example_translation: What I like most about Mexico is the people.

example_sentence: El escritor cuyo libro leí ganó el Nobel.
example_translation: The writer whose book I read won the Nobel.

example_sentence: Los que lleguen tarde no entrarán.
example_translation: Those who arrive late won't get in.

example_sentence: El amigo con quien viajé es muy buena onda.
example_translation: The friend with whom I traveled is really cool.

example_sentence: Resulta que ella es la hermana de mi vecino.
example_translation: It turns out she's my neighbor's sister.

example_sentence: El lugar donde nos conocimos es muy especial.
example_translation: The place where we met is very special.

translation: what I like / what I enjoy
example_sentence: Lo que me gusta de Oaxaca es el mole y la arquitectura.
example_translation: What I like about Oaxaca is the mole and the architecture.

translation: the one who / the one that
example_sentence: La que llegó primero ganó el premio.
example_translation: The one who arrived first won the prize.

example_sentence: La persona a quien llamé no contestó.
example_translation: The person whom I called didn't answer.
